note: Thursday 5/14 daily scrape covering the 5/13-5/14 window. 9 new leads scoring 60+ across 4 pipelines (6 pr-marketing, 2 fund-formation, 1 legal-freelance, 1 ai-consulting). The 5/12-5/13 funding stretch stayed heavy: yesterday's scrape already captured the 5/12 standouts (Greenboard, Adfin, Vapi) so those are excluded here as dedup. Today's pickups come from the remainder of the 5/12 board plus the full 5/13 board. TOP NEW LEAD is A3/C PARTNERS (fund-formation) — a textbook emerging-manager ICP hit: ex-Warburg Pincus head of Europe + ex-Inflexion partner registered a debut tech-focused PE fund in May 2026 and are halfway to a £300M/$403M target. On pr-marketing, the strongest fits are EXAFORCE ($125M Series B AI cybersecurity, ~$725M valuation), ELLIPTIC ($120M Series D crypto-compliance for FIs, Nasdaq + Deutsche Bank backing), and OUTMARKET AI ($17M Series A AI for insurance brokerages) — note Exaforce and Elliptic both carry meaningful PR-incumbency risk given their valuations, so they are scored B not A despite strong surface metrics. BRANCHLAB and JUDGMENT LABS round out pr-marketing as solid-but-secondary B leads. Legal-freelance produced one clean contract posting (Major Lindsey & Africa-placed Chief Corporate & Sustainability Counsel, $133/hr contract, remote). DesignVerse is a small-seed ai-consulting B lead. Job-board overnight churn for freelance pipelines was light: Indeed's investment-fund-counsel and communications-fractional remote feeds surfaced mostly recruiter/staffing-firm aggregator listings and full-time wealth-advisory roles, not net-new freelance-counsel or PR-consultant company postings — the one qualifying posting (MLA Chief Corporate Counsel) is included. SIGNAL REFRESH: Greenboard's GreenboardGo product launch is confirmed and live (press cycle still OPEN); Adfin confirmed an engineering+sales hiring ramp and reconfirmed its Scaling Europe award (window OPEN); Vapi, Embat, and Nova Intelligence are all in natural post-funding score decay. AUTONOMOUS-RUN NOTES: (1) scored Exaforce/Elliptic as B-tier rather than A despite 78-80 raw combined scores, because both are large, high-valuation companies that very likely already retain PR or are mid-RFP — flagged as incumbency risk in their notes; (2) included the MLA-placed contract counsel posting even though MLA already exists as a tracked staffing entity, because the routing rules treat individual contract postings as their own leads; (3) Comfi ($65M B2B BNPL) surfaced in search but was excluded — its raise was announced 4/27, outside the 24-48h window.

leads.name: A3/C Partners
leads.industry: Emerging-manager private equity firm — debut technology-focused buyout/growth fund. Founded by Adarsh Sarma (former head of Europe, Warburg Pincus) and Ed Lynch (former partner, Inflexion). London-based.
leads.fundingStage: Debut fund (Fund I) registered May 2026. Targeting £300M+; reported to have raised approximately £300M ($403M) and is roughly halfway to its target. Actively in-market.

contacts.name: Adarsh Sarma
contacts.title: Co-Founder (former Head of Europe, Warburg Pincus)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (With Intelligence — Private Equity Emerging Managers to Watch in 2026)
leads.notes: TOP NEW LEAD this cycle (combined 82, A-tier) and the cleanest fund-formation ICP hit in weeks. Three reasons: (a) textbook emerging-manager profile — a debut Fund I from a credentialed team (ex-Warburg Pincus head of Europe + ex-Inflexion partner) is exactly the buyer with enough deal complexity to pay for judgment but not yet a deep in-house legal bench, (b) the fund is mid-raise — registered May 2026, ~£300M of a £300M+ target already secured, halfway home — which means there is live, recurring fund-formation and fund-maintenance legal work right now (LP/GP side letters with each new closing, subscription docs, regulatory filings, governance), (c) founder pedigree means ability-to-pay is essentially uncapped — this is a credible, well-capitalized launch, not a hopeful first-timer. CAVEAT: a firm this far into its raise has almost certainly engaged formation counsel for the core fund vehicle already — the realistic wedge is ongoing fund-maintenance / closing-by-closing work, side letters, co-invest vehicles, and (critically) being positioned early for Fund II. Pitch angle: position as fractional / outside fund counsel for the closing cadence and side-letter volume of the current raise, with an eye to becoming the standing relationship before Fund II. UK jurisdiction — confirm Mark's comfort with UK/cross-border fund structuring or whether this is a referral-out. Outreach: warm intro through PE/LP network strongly preferred over cold; Sarma and Lynch are both highly networked. Time-sensitive: closings happen on a rolling basis during the raise — earlier is better.
leads.lastActivity: Debut technology-focused PE fund registered May 2026; approximately £300M ($403M) raised against target, roughly halfway to goal, actively in-market (With Intelligence emerging-managers coverage)

leads.name: Exaforce
leads.industry: AI-native cybersecurity — autonomous security operations platform using AI 'Exabots' to triage and stop threats in real time. B2B enterprise software.
leads.fundingStage: $125M Series B announced May 12, 2026 at approximately $725M valuation. Investors: HarbourVest (lead), Peak XV Partners, Mayfield, Khosla Ventures, Seligman Ventures. Follows a $75M Series A; total funding approximately $200M. Founded ~2023.

contacts.name: Exaforce CEO (verify exact name from 5/12 HarbourVest / funding coverage)
contacts.title: Co-founder / CEO

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Tech Startups — Top Startup and Tech Funding News, May 12, 2026)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 79, just under the A line). Exaforce has strong surface metrics — $125M Series B, ~$725M valuation, ~$200M total raised, a tier-1 cap table (HarbourVest / Peak XV / Mayfield / Khosla), and a textbook B2B enterprise sales motion selling an autonomous SOC platform. It also rides a live news tailwind (the 'first AI-orchestrated cyberattack' story is driving enterprise-security press right now). DELIBERATELY SCORED B, NOT A, despite the 79: a 3-year-old company at a $725M valuation with this cap table almost certainly already retains a PR firm or is mid-RFP — the dm_engaged and responsive sub-scores are held low to reflect that incumbency risk. This is the same caution we applied to Vapi (5/13). Worth pursuing only if Mark wants to compete for an enterprise-security account against likely incumbents — the realistic angle is a specialist FS/regulated-industry-security narrative thread (selling Exabots into banks and financial institutions) rather than a generalist retained pitch. Pitch threading if pursued: (i) enterprise security trade press (Dark Reading, SC Media, CyberScoop, The Record), (ii) the FS-security-buyer angle for financial-services trade press, (iii) CEO thought-leadership on autonomous SOC / agentic security. Outreach: confirm whether they already have PR representation before investing pitch effort. Combined 79 — B-tier, pursue selectively.
leads.lastActivity: $125M Series B announced May 12, 2026 at ~$725M valuation (HarbourVest lead; Peak XV, Mayfield, Khosla, Seligman participating — Tech Startups funding roundup)

leads.name: Elliptic
leads.industry: Blockchain analytics and crypto compliance — transaction screening and risk analytics for financial institutions, banks, and regulators. B2B SaaS / RegTech.
leads.fundingStage: $120M Series D announced May 12, 2026 at approximately $670M valuation. Investors: One Peak (lead), Nasdaq Ventures, Deutsche Bank, British Business Bank.
leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Tech Startups — Elliptic raises $120M coverage)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 78). Elliptic is a near-perfect industry-and-buyer fit on paper: crypto compliance and blockchain analytics sold to banks, financial institutions, and regulators sits squarely in Mark's FS-regulatory-adjacent sweet spot, and the cap table is exceptional — Nasdaq Ventures and Deutsche Bank as strategic investors is about as institutionally credible as it gets. DELIBERATELY SCORED B, NOT A: this is a Series D company at a $670M valuation — it has had an in-house communications function for years and well-established media relationships. The 'trigger' here (a late-stage growth round) is real news but does NOT create the same greenfield retained-PR opening that a Series A with no in-house comms does. dm_engaged and responsive sub-scores are held low accordingly. Realistic value of this lead: NOT a retained-PR pitch — more likely a project or advisory engagement (e.g., a specific campaign around a product launch, a regulatory-moment thought-leadership push, or US-market expansion support) layered alongside their existing comms team. CEO Simone Maini is publicly named and press-active. Pitch angle if pursued: project-based earned-media support for a defined expansion or regulatory milestone, positioned as overflow/specialist capacity, not as replacing an incumbent. Combined 78 — B-tier, pursue only as a project/advisory opportunity.
leads.lastActivity: $120M Series D announced May 12, 2026 at ~$670M valuation (One Peak lead; Nasdaq Ventures, Deutsche Bank, British Business Bank participating — Tech Startups)

contacts.name: Outmarket AI CEO (verify exact name from 5/13 funding coverage)
contacts.title: Co-founder / CEO

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Tech Startups — Top Startup and Tech Funding News, May 13, 2026)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 78) and, of today's pr-marketing pickups, the BEST true retained-PR fit — better than the higher-profile Exaforce/Elliptic for Mark's actual business model. Reasoning: (a) it is a clean Series A ($17M, ~$21.7M total) — the stage where companies have a real budget but no in-house comms function yet, which is the greenfield retained-PR opening Mark wants, (b) InsurTech is solidly FS-adjacent and the buyer is crisp: insurance brokerages, a relationship-heavy, under-digitized market that responds well to earned-media credibility, (c) TTV Capital on the cap table is a fintech-specialist signal — TTV portfolio companies reliably invest in PR/comms post-Series A. Buyer fit 41/50: InsurTech (strong FS-adjacency), Series A growth stage, very clear B2B sales motion to brokerages. Intent 37/50: fresh funding trigger, press cycle day 1, expansion underway. Pitch threading: (i) insurance trade press (Insurance Journal, Digital Insurance, Insurance Business America, PropertyCasualty360), (ii) InsurTech / fintech press (Coverager, InsurTech Insights, FinTech Global), (iii) the 'AI workflow layer for brokerages' category narrative, (iv) CEO thought-leadership on insurance-distribution modernization. Outreach: verify CEO name from 5/13 coverage, reach out in next 48-72h while the funding news peg is fresh. Combined 78 — B-tier, pursue as a priority retained-PR target.
leads.lastActivity: $17M Series A announced May 13, 2026 (Permanent Capital Ventures, SignalFire, Fika Ventures, TTV Capital, Dash Fund — Tech Startups funding roundup)

leads.name: BranchLab
leads.industry: AI platform for pharmaceutical commercialization — privacy-first AI covering patient identification, audience segmentation, activation, and real-world measurement. B2B SaaS / HealthTech.
leads.fundingStage: $26M Series A announced May 13, 2026. Investors: McKesson Ventures (lead), FCA Venture Partners, Sanofi Ventures, AIX Ventures. Total funding $35M.

contacts.name: BranchLab CEO (verify exact name from 5/13 McKesson Ventures coverage)
contacts.title: Co-founder / CEO

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Tech Startups — BranchLab raises $26M coverage)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 73). BranchLab is a solid Series A pickup with a strong intent profile — fresh $26M raise, strategic backing from McKesson Ventures (lead) and Sanofi Ventures, and a clean B2B sales motion into pharma. The drag on the score is industry fit: pharma commercialization sits OUTSIDE Mark's core FS/Tech ICP (industry sub-score held to 5/10). That said, the company is a privacy-first AI B2B SaaS play, the stage is right (Series A, likely no in-house comms), and the strategic-investor cap table signals retained-PR readiness. Worth pursuing if Mark is open to a healthcare-adjacent account; the press surface (pharma-marketing and healthtech trade press — Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News, MM+M, Digital Commerce 360 Healthcare) is a different network than Mark's FS bread-and-butter, so factor in relationship-building cost. Pitch threading: (i) pharma-marketing trade press, (ii) healthtech/AI press, (iii) the privacy-first-AI angle for data-governance and AI-ethics coverage, (iv) CEO thought-leadership on pharma launch execution. Combined 73 — B-tier, pursue if healthcare-adjacent accounts are in scope.
leads.lastActivity: $26M Series A announced May 13, 2026 (McKesson Ventures lead; FCA Venture Partners, Sanofi Ventures, AIX Ventures participating — Tech Startups)

leads.name: Judgment Labs
leads.industry: AI infrastructure — platform that turns production data from AI applications into actionable fixes, creating a continuous feedback loop to improve AI agents over time. B2B developer/AI infrastructure.
leads.fundingStage: $32M combined Seed and Series A announced May 12, 2026. Lightspeed Venture Partners led both rounds; participation from Nova Global, SV Angel, Valor Equity Partners, Dynamic. Total funding $32M.
leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Tech Startups — Judgment Labs $32M coverage)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 70). Judgment Labs is an early-stage AI-infrastructure play with a strong investor signal — Lightspeed led BOTH the seed and Series A and doubled down just six months after its first check, which is a meaningful conviction marker. Founders (Alex Shan, Joseph Camyre, Andrew Li) are all publicly named and press-accessible. The score is held in B-tier because: (a) the product is deep-infrastructure AI tooling, a harder earned-media story than a clean enterprise-app narrative, (b) the AI-agent-optimization space is crowded and noisy, making differentiation work harder, (c) at $32M total the budget tier is real but modest. Still a legitimate retained-PR target: the stage is right (no in-house comms), the cap table is credible, and 'making AI agents reliable' is a narrative with genuine press appetite. Pitch threading: (i) AI infrastructure / developer press (The Information, VentureBeat AI, The New Stack, SiliconANGLE), (ii) the 'AI agent reliability' category narrative, (iii) founder thought-leadership (Chief Scientist Andrew Li is a natural technical-credibility voice). DUAL-PIPELINE NOTE: also a plausible ai-consulting prospect if Mark wants to pitch advisory services rather than PR — but primary route is pr-marketing. Combined 70 — B-tier, pursue.
leads.lastActivity: $32M combined Seed and Series A announced May 12, 2026 (Lightspeed led both rounds; Nova Global, SV Angel, Valor Equity, Dynamic participating)

leads.name: Major, Lindsey & Africa — Chief Corporate & Sustainability Counsel (Contract, Remote)
leads.industry: Contract in-house counsel placement (via Major, Lindsey & Africa) — primary legal counsel for corporate governance and compliance, including governance of an organization with 80+ global subsidiaries. End buyer is a multi-entity operating company.
leads.fundingStage: N/A — contract legal placement. Posted via Major, Lindsey & Africa (legal staffing/search firm). $133/hour, Contract.

contacts.name: Major, Lindsey & Africa — Interim Legal Talent recruiter (verify from posting)
contacts.title: Legal Recruiter / Placement Contact

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (Indeed — investment fund counsel remote jobs feed, MLA-placed posting)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 70). A clean contract-counsel posting placed through Major, Lindsey & Africa: Chief Corporate & Sustainability Counsel, $133/hour, Contract, remote, serving as primary legal counsel for governance and compliance across an organization with 80+ global subsidiaries. Bucket-test (4-bucket taxonomy): Bucket A (contract) + Bucket B (interim/external via staffing) + Bucket C (counsel/specialist) — hits 3 of 4 buckets, qualifies cleanly. Fit 30/50: no fund/private-funds component (0), but a strong corporate-governance practice-area match (10), Chief Counsel seniority match (10), and a stated, market-credible comp (10). Intent 40/50 is the strength of this lead — it is an explicit contract engagement (15), remote (13), with a direct placement path through MLA (7). Suppression check: no red flags — not litigation, not trusts & estates, not court-appearance work; corporate governance/compliance is in-scope. NOTE: MLA already exists in the CRM as a tracked staffing/talent entity, but per the routing rules an individual contract posting is its own lead — this is the specific placement, not the firm. The 80+-global-subsidiary governance scope signals a substantial, ongoing workload that could convert to a longer fractional GC arrangement. Pitch angle: Mark applies/expresses interest through the MLA Interim Legal Talent channel, positioning as fractional corporate governance counsel. Combined 70 — B-tier, pursue via MLA placement channel.
leads.lastActivity: Active contract posting on Indeed (Chief Corporate & Sustainability Counsel, $133/hr, Contract, remote) placed via Major, Lindsey & Africa Interim Legal Talent

leads.name: Atlas Health Capital
leads.industry: Emerging-manager private equity firm focused on the mid-market healthcare space — investing in medical technology, niche pharma, and animal health sub-sectors. Founded by Ben Long.
leads.fundingStage: Emerging manager / early-fund launch (cited among notable recent emerging-manager launches in 2026). Specific fund size and close timeline not yet disclosed in available coverage.
leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-14 (With Intelligence — Private Equity Emerging Managers to Watch in 2026)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 65). Atlas Health Capital is a legitimate emerging-manager fund-formation prospect — a new mid-market healthcare PE firm (medtech, niche pharma, animal health) founded by Ben Long, cited among notable 2026 emerging-manager launches. It scores below A3/C Partners because the available coverage is thin: no disclosed fund size, no stated close timeline, and limited operational-readiness detail — the intent sub-scores are conservative as a result and should be revised once more is known. The fit is real, though: emerging healthcare-PE manager is exactly the buyer profile that needs fund-formation counsel without a deep in-house bench. NEXT STEP for verification: confirm fund stage (is Fund I actually in-market or still pre-launch?), target size, and whether formation counsel is already engaged — this determines whether the realistic engagement is core formation work or downstream maintenance. Pitch angle: position early as outside fund-formation counsel for a first-time healthcare-PE manager; the earlier in the launch, the larger the formation scope. Combined 65 — B-tier, pursue after verification.
leads.lastActivity: Cited among notable 2026 emerging-manager launches — mid-market healthcare PE firm founded by Ben Long (With Intelligence emerging-managers coverage)

leads.notes: B-tier (combined 65). DesignVerse is a small-seed ai-consulting prospect with a genuinely strong proof point — it modernized a 15-year-old mission-critical aviation application used by EUROCONTROL in just over a month versus a conventional ~6 months. The regulated-industries focus and an LSEG angel investor give it some FS-adjacency. Scored in B-tier because: (a) at $5.5M seed / $6.35M total, the budget tier is modest, (b) it is early — buyer access (CIO/CTO) and internal-sponsor signals are unproven, (c) the company itself sells AI software, so the ai-consulting angle is about Mark advising THEM or co-selling, not a classic consulting buyer. The realistic value here is a relationship-building / advisory-positioning play rather than a near-term engagement. NEXT STEP: verify CEO name and whether the company is open to advisory partnerships. Combined 65 — B-tier, pursue as a relationship/advisory-development lead, low urgency.

The GreenboardGo launch confirmation gives a layered press story (funding + product) and a clean retained-PR thread. Email CEO Dave Feldman direct; lead with the SEC/FINRA compliance-trade-press angle (Compliance Week, RIABiz, WealthManagement.com) Mark already has relationships in.
